e_visa.nationality: 87 countries listed on evisa.imigrasi.go.id including Albania, Andorra, Argentina, Australia, Austria, Bahrain, Belarus, Belgium, Bosnia Herzegovina, Brazil, Brunei Darussalam, Bulgaria, Cambodia, Canada, Chile, China, Colombia, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech, Denmark, Ecuador, Egypt, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hong Kong, Hungary, Iceland, India, Ireland, Italy, Japan, Jordan, Kuwait, Kazakhstan, Laos, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malaysia, Maldives, Malta, Mexico, Monaco, Morocco, Myanmar, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Oman, Palestine, Peru, Philippines, Poland, Portugal, Republic of Korea, Qatar, Romania, Russia, San Marino, Saudi Arabia, Serbia, Seychelles, Singapore, Slovakia, Slovenia, South Africa, Spain, Suriname, Sweden, Switzerland, Taiwan, Thailand, Timor Leste, Tunisia, Turkiye, Ukraine, United Arab Emirates, United Kingdom, United States of America, Uzbekistan, Vatican, Vietnam
e_visa.notes: Visitor Visa (e-Visa) via evisa.imigrasi.go.id. Single-entry. Visa validity 90 days from issuance; maximum stay is 30 days from arrival. Cost IDR 500,000. Purposes: tourism, government visits, business meetings, goods purchasing, or transit. Requires credit card (Mastercard, Visa, or JCB). Available at 16 international airports, 95+ seaports, 7 cross-border posts.

visa_policy.notes: Indonesia has three main entry categories for foreign ordinary passport holders: (1) BVK - Bebas Visa Kunjungan (visa-free visit) for 16 nationalities/entities, max 30 days non-extendable, no fee; (2) VoA - Visa on Arrival for 97 countries, cost IDR 500,000, 30 days extendable once; (3) Visitor Visa (e-Visa) via evisa.imigrasi.go.id for 87 countries, 30 days stay, cost IDR 500,000. The official imigrasi.go.id VoA country list as re-verified on 2026-06-13 confirms 97 countries including Canada (Kanada) and Sweden (Swedia) which were missing from the prior record. Presidential Regulation 95/2024 resumed visa-free (BVK) access for Colombia, Hong Kong, and Suriname. BVK countries (16) are distinct from VoA: they enter free of charge, non-extendable; the VoA list on the official site includes all 97 nationalities which also appear to encompass some BVK-eligible nationalities on the VoA page but BVK is the more favourable instrument.

visa_types.name: Visa on Arrival (VOA / B211A)
visa_types.notes: Available to nationals of 90+ countries at designated entry points including major international airports and seaports. IDR 500,000 (approx. USD 35) fee. Single-entry, 30-day stay, extendable once for 30 days at immigration office (IDR 500,000). Also available as e-VOA (paid online in advance) via molina.imigrasi.go.id. B211A visa code.

visa_types.name: Social Budaya Visa (B211 — Social/Cultural)
visa_types.purpose: Social/cultural activities, tourism, family visit, language study, short-term courses
visa_types.notes: Applied online via Indonesian immigration portal or at Indonesian embassies/consulates. Requires Indonesian citizen/legal entity as guarantor (penjamin). 60-day stay, extendable up to 4 times (each extension 30 days, max 180 days total in-country). Commonly used by digital nomads and long-stay tourists who cannot use VOA extensions. B211 visa code.

visa_types.name: Second Home Visa (E33G — Digital Nomad/Remote Worker)
visa_types.purpose: Long-term remote work and residency in Indonesia
visa_types.notes: 5-year multiple-entry visa for digital nomads and remote workers. Requires proof of funds: IDR 2 billion (approx. USD 130,000) in bank account or investments, or ownership of property in Indonesia at equivalent value. Not a work permit — holder may not be employed by Indonesian entity or earn income from Indonesian sources. Applied online. E33G visa code. Each stay up to 1 year, renewable within 5-year validity.

visa_types.name: Retirement KITAS (Kartu Izin Tinggal Terbatas)
visa_types.notes: For retirees aged 55+. Requirements: monthly pension income of minimum USD 1,500/month demonstrated via bank statements; health insurance; proof of adequate housing in Indonesia; guarantor letter. KITAS valid 1 year, renewable up to 5 consecutive years before converting to KITAP (permanent residency). No work authorization; may employ 1 Indonesian domestic helper.

visa_types.name: Work KITAS / TA-01 Work Visa
visa_types.notes: Foreign worker KITAS. Requires RPTKA (foreign worker placement plan) from employer and NOTIFIKASI (notification) from Ministry of Manpower (Kemnaker). Employer must pay USD 100/month expatriate levy (DKPTKA). KITAS valid up to 2 years, renewable. Work KITAS holder may sponsor spouse/children for Dependent KITAS. Some positions restricted for Indonesian nationals only.

outbound_official.notes: Source is the official Tasikmalaya Immigration Office (Kantor Imigrasi Kelas I Non TPI Tasikmalaya), a regional office of Indonesia's Directorate General of Immigration (imigrasi.go.id). The page lists 45 visa-free, 32 visa-on-arrival, and 2 eTA destinations for Indonesian ordinary passport holders. No date was published on the source page. This is origin-side (outbound) data — what Indonesian citizens can access. The main imigrasi.go.id and kemlu.go.id central portals do not appear to maintain a single consolidated outbound page; this regional immigration office page is the best official origin-side source located.

cbi.notes: Indonesia does not offer a Citizenship by Investment (CBI) program. Indonesia does not generally allow dual citizenship for adults. The Golden Visa and Global Citizen of Indonesia (GCI) programs provide long-term residency only, not citizenship. The GCI was launched in January 2026 as a permanent residency alternative for diaspora and spouses of Indonesians, but does not confer citizenship. Source: imigrasi.go.id, bandung.imigrasi.go.id

programs.name: Golden Visa Indonesia (E28C)
programs.notes: Established via Ministerial Regulation No. 22/2023 (Law and Human Rights) and No. 82/2023 (Finance), effective 30 August 2023. Grants 5 or 10-year stay permits. Categories: (1) Individual investor without establishing company (financial placement in bonds/stocks/deposits): USD 350,000 for 5 years, USD 700,000 for 10 years; (2) Individual investor establishing company: USD 2,500,000 for 5 years, USD 5,000,000 for 10 years; (3) Corporate investor director/commissioner: USD 25,000,000 for 5 years, USD 50,000,000 for 10 years. No path to citizenship or permanent residency via this route. Average processing: 4 business days. Applications via evisa.imigrasi.go.id (Molina portal).

programs.name: Second Home Visa (Visa Rumah Kedua)
programs.notes: Launched October 25, 2022 via Circular Letter IMI-0740.GR.01.01 of 2022. Allows foreign nationals or ex-Indonesian citizens to reside in Indonesia for 5 or 10 years. Requires proof of funds minimum IDR 2,000,000,000 (approx. USD 125,000) in bank account. Visa fee IDR 3,000,000. Passport validity minimum 36 months required. No sponsor required. Application via visa-online.imigrasi.go.id. No explicit path to permanent residence or citizenship.

programs.name: E33G Second Home Visa for Remote Workers (Digital Nomad Visa)
programs.notes: E33G Visa Rumah Kedua Pekerja Jarak Jauh. 1-year stay, extendable online. Minimum annual income: USD 60,000. Proof of living expenses: minimum USD 2,000 or equivalent (last 3 months bank statement). Employment contract with foreign-registered company required. No local sponsor required. Processing: 5 business days. Visa fee: Rp 7,000,000 for 1-year residence. Applications via evisa.imigrasi.go.id.

programs.name: Global Citizen of Indonesia (GCI)
programs.notes: Launched January 2026 by Ministry of Immigration and Corrections. Grants unlimited/permanent stay permit for eligible foreign nationals: former Indonesian citizens and their descendants (up to 2nd degree), spouses of Indonesian citizens, and those in international marriages involving Indonesians. Modest investment requirements (bonds, shares, mutual funds, property) or income of USD 1,500/month or USD 15,000/year. Diaspora with strategic expertise exempt from investment requirements. Reporting required every 5 years only. Applications via evisa.imigrasi.go.id.

programs.name: Golden Visa Indonesia - Global Talent Category
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals with globally recognized talent, expertise, or achievement in fields valuable to Indonesia's economy
programs.notes: Part of the Golden Visa framework (E28C). Categories include global talent, personage, repatriation (former Indonesian citizens), and IKN (Nusantara Capital) investor. Specific financial thresholds for talent category were referenced on imigrasi.go.id but not enumerated separately from investment categories on available pages.

programs.name: E33G Visa Rumah Kedua Pekerja Jarak Jauh (Remote Worker / Digital Nomad Visa)
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals employed by a company established outside Indonesia, with minimum annual income of USD 60,000 and minimum savings/expenses of USD 2,000
programs.notes: 1-year stay, extendable. No local sponsor required. Must not receive compensation from Indonesian entities. Fee: Rp 7,000,000. Apply via evisa.imigrasi.go.id.

agreements: ASEAN (visa-free entry for all 9 other ASEAN member state nationals)
PBVDD - Perjanjian Bebas Visa Diplomatik dan Dinas (Diplomatic and Service Passport Visa Exemption Agreements) with 101 countries as of 12 August 2024

conditional_access.conditions: Diplomatic and service/official passport holders from 101 PBVDD partner countries are visa-exempt for visits up to 30 days under bilateral Perjanjian Bebas Visa Diplomatik dan Dinas (PBVDD) agreements. The list as of 12 August 2024 covers: Albania, Angola, Antigua & Barbuda, Argentina, Armenia, Austria, Azerbaijan, Bahrain, Bangladesh, Belarus, Belgium, Bosnia & Herzegovina, Brazil, Brunei Darussalam, Bulgaria, Burundi, Cambodia, Chile, China (PRC), Colombia, Costa Rica, Croatia, Cuba, Czechia, Denmark (e-passport only), Ecuador, Egypt, El Salvador, Equatorial Guinea, Ethiopia, Fiji, Hungary, India, Iran, Italy, Japan, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Kuwait, Kyrgyzstan, Laos, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malaysia, Mexico, Moldova, Mongolia, Montenegro, Morocco, Mozambique, Myanmar, Namibia, Netherlands, Nicaragua, Niger, North Korea, North Macedonia, Norway, Pakistan, Panama, Papua New Guinea, Paraguay, Peru, Philippines, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Russia, Rwanda, Saint Kitts & Nevis, Senegal, Serbia, Slovakia, Slovenia, Solomon Islands, South Korea, Sri Lanka, Suriname, Switzerland, Ukraine, United Arab Emirates, Vietnam, Greece. NOTE: Ireland — diplomatic passport only. United Kingdom — diplomatic passport only. Denmark — e-passport only. Sweden — e-passport only. Holders must carry a Diplomatic Note of assignment or visit notification from their Foreign Ministry.
Ireland: PBVDD visa exemption applies to diplomatic passport holders ONLY (not service/official passport). Per official kemlu.go.id PBVDD brochure dated 12 August 2024.
United Kingdom: PBVDD visa exemption applies to diplomatic passport holders ONLY (not service/official passport). Per official kemlu.go.id PBVDD brochure dated 12 August 2024.

Foreign nationals holding Singapore permanent residency (PR) status are eligible for BVK (visa-free entry), provided they enter through designated Immigration Checkpoint Authority posts. This is explicitly listed as item 17 on the official BVK subject list at imigrasi.go.id.

validation_notes: 2026-06-13: Re-verified against live official imigrasi.go.id and evisa.imigrasi.go.id pages.
ADDED Canada (CAN) to visa_on_arrival: confirmed as #37 (Kanada) on the live imigrasi.go.id VoA list. Was entirely absent from the previous record.
ADDED Sweden (SWE) to visa_on_arrival: confirmed as #81 (Swedia) on the live imigrasi.go.id VoA list. Was missing from the previous record's VoA section.
REMOVED duplicate Brazil entry from visa_on_arrival: Brazil appears on the BVK (visa-free) list and should not be duplicated in VoA. Retained in visa_free only.

CORRECTED source_url for all BVK visa_free entries: changed from jogja.imigrasi.go.id regional page to the primary official imigrasi.go.id/wna/daftar-negara-voa-bvk-calling-visa/daftar-negara-bebas-visa-kunjungan page.

CORRECTED source_url for Kenya and Rwanda VoA entries: updated to primary imigrasi.go.id VoA list (both confirmed present on live official list) instead of regional jogja.imigrasi.go.id announcement page.
UPDATED conditional_access Singapore PR source_url to primary imigrasi.go.id BVK page (official list item #17 explicitly references Singapore PR holders).

UPDATED e-visa country list notes: expanded to enumerate all 87 eligible countries as listed on evisa.imigrasi.go.id/front/info/evoa (live, re-fetched 2026-06-13). Canada and Sweden confirmed present.
e-visa portal no longer references a separate 'e-VoA via VFS Global' product in its FAQ — removed that reference from notes.
VoA official page total remains 97 countries, consistent with prior record.
gaps: The complete official calling visa country list (Negara Calling Visa) was not retrieved or verified.
Golden Visa talent and personage category-specific investment thresholds not found verbatim on official pages.
Indonesia's dual citizenship law specifics not retrieved from official legislative source; dual citizenship=false confirmed indirectly via imigrasi.go.id GCI descriptions.
The E28C Golden Visa sub-categories for 'silver hair' and 'IKN investor' minimum investment amounts not found verbatim on scraped official pages.

No official Indonesian government source was found confirming visa-free or VoA entry privileges for ordinary passport holders based solely on holding a third-country visa or residence permit (e.g., US visa, Schengen visa, UK visa, Australian visa, GCC residence, or OCI card).
The full PBVDD 101-country list was reconstructed from the kemlu.go.id PDF (12 August 2024); some countries in the bilingual two-column layout may have been missed.
Denmark and Sweden PBVDD exemption scope (whether it applies to both diplomatic and service passports or only one type) was not further clarified in the available official source.

The VoA list page shows 97 countries but several BVK-eligible nationalities (e.g., Brunei, Malaysia, Singapore, Thailand, Vietnam, Philippines, Cambodia, Myanmar, Laos, Timor-Leste, Hong Kong, Colombia, Suriname, Turkey, Peru) also appear on the VoA page — the precise relationship (whether BVK nations can also use VoA at cost, or whether the VoA list just happens to include them as a superset) was not explicitly clarified on the official source.
